name: Visit Timepoint Assignment
shortDescription: Convert longitudinal visit dates into study timepoints (T1, T2, ...) using CBR TLSA/SANSCOG window rules.
description: Takes a longitudinal cohort table with one row per visit and assigns each visit a study timepoint. For every participant the earliest visit becomes the baseline; each later visit's elapsed time from baseline is matched against the cohort's timepoint window schedule (TLSA: annual, SANSCOG: biennial). When several visits fall in the same window, one is kept according to the chosen rule and the collision is reported. Emits the timepointed table plus a duplicate-visit QC report.
inputDescription: One tabular file (parquet, csv or xlsx) with one row per visit. Must contain a participant identifier column and a visit date column.

name.label: Name
name.description: Unique identifier for this node in the pipeline

scheme.label: Timepoint schedule
scheme.description: Annual (TLSA), biennial (SANSCOG), or your own window bounds.

participant_column.label: Participant ID column
participant_column.description: Baseline and timepoints are computed per participant.

date_column.label: Visit date column
date_column.description: Rows with a missing or unparseable date are dropped and logged.

cohort_column.label: Cohort column
cohort_column.description: Optional. Filter to a single cohort before assigning.

cohort_value.label: Cohort value
cohort_value.description: Keep only rows matching this cohort, e.g. TLSA.

collision_rule.label: When two visits land in one window
collision_rule.description: Applies from T2 onward; T1 is always the earliest visit. Collisions are reported either way.

options.label: Keep the visit closest to the ideal window centre

promote_late_baseline.label: Promote a late baseline visit to T2
promote_late_baseline.description: Relabel a late T1 visit as T2 when T2 is empty and an earlier visit remains as T1.

promote_window_fraction.label: Late-baseline promotion band
promote_window_fraction.description: How close to the T1/T2 boundary a visit must be, as a fraction of the T1 window.

days_per_year.label: Days per time unit
days_per_year.description: Days in one unit of elapsed time. 365.25 = years (leap-adjusted), 365 = the original analysis, 30.44 = months, 7 = weeks, 1 = days.

max_timepoints.label: Maximum timepoints
max_timepoints.description: Visits beyond the last window are left unassigned and logged.

custom_windows.label: Custom window definition
custom_windows.description: Only when schedule is Custom. Repeating interval: interval_years, t1_max, t2_min, t2_max, lower_margin, upper_margin. Irregular schedule: {"windows": [[min,max], ...]}, one pair per timepoint in order.

drop_unassigned.label: Drop unassigned visits
drop_unassigned.description: Remove visits outside every window and collision losers. Off keeps them with a blank timepoint.

tolerance_years.label: Protocol tolerance (years)
tolerance_years.description: How far from the scheduled anniversary still counts as on-schedule. 0.25 is the CBR +/-3 month window. Only flags visits; nothing is dropped.

outputDescription: Timepointed visit table with each visit's deviation from its scheduled anniversary, plus a QC report of visits that collided within a window.
